title: Identify The Hospital Controller As The Component Used To Remove And Add Totes
summary: Use the training overview slide and transcript to verify that the hospital controller is the component identified in this source as being used to remove and add totes.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when a user needs to identify which component on the OptiSweep overview architecture is described in the training source as handling tote removal and tote addition.
not_for: Do not use this as a detailed operating procedure for physically removing or adding totes.
Do not use this to infer button presses, HMI workflow, controller commands, or recovery actions not stated in the source.
Do not use this to determine AGV, tote tip station, or OptiSweep Software operating responsibilities beyond what is explicitly shown on the overview slide.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the training slide and transcript to identify the component named in the source.

safety_notes: This source is a component-identification reference only.
Do not infer or perform tote add/remove operations from this segment alone.
Escalate if a detailed operating procedure is required because this source does not provide action steps, controls, or screen workflow for tote handling.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training video segment or extracted slide

access_required: Access to the training source segment training_video_day1_segment_0005
Access to the extracted training slide artifact
system_preconditions: The user can view the overview architecture slide and associated transcript from the training source.

visual_references.description: Overview training slide showing the core OptiSweep system elements, including the hospital controller label among other architecture components.

steps.title: Locate the hospital controller on the overview slide
steps.purpose: Identify the hospital controller as a labeled component on the source architecture diagram.
steps.instruction: Open the overview training slide and locate the hospital controller label in the architecture diagram.
steps.expected_result: The hospital controller is visible as one of the labeled core elements on the slide.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can visually identify the hospital controller label on the overview slide.
steps.failure_condition: The hospital controller label cannot be located on the provided slide or the artifact is unavailable.

actions.label: Review overview architecture slide
actions.exact_action: Open the overview training slide and locate the hospital controller in the architecture diagram.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the hospital controller label among the core elements shown with UPS Chat, tote tipper or tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, OptiSweep software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the overview slide cannot be accessed.
Escalate if the hospital controller label is not legible in the available artifact.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Training slide showing the core OptiSweep system elements and how OptiSweep connects UPS Chat, tote handling equipment, AGVs, hospital controller, sorter, and HMI.
Overview slide shows UPS Chat, tote tipper/tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI; transcript also mentions scan tunnel.

steps.title: Confirm the transcript statement about tote handling
steps.purpose: Verify the source statement assigning tote removal and tote addition to the hospital controller.
steps.instruction: Confirm from the transcript that the hospital controller is described as the component used to remove and add totes.
steps.expected_result: The transcript explicitly states that the hospital controller is for removing and adding totes.
steps.healthy_condition: The user finds the direct statement linking the hospital controller to tote removal and tote addition.
steps.failure_condition: The transcript does not contain the statement or the user cannot verify the wording from the source.

actions.label: Review transcript statement
actions.exact_action: Confirm from the transcript that the hospital controller is described as the component used to remove and add totes.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the transcript is unavailable.
Escalate if a more detailed tote handling procedure is needed, because this source only identifies the component role.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We have a hospital controller for you to remove and add totes.
The hospital controller is identified in this training segment as the component used to remove and add totes.

steps.title: Differentiate the hospital controller from other components
steps.purpose: Avoid confusing the hospital controller with other labeled elements on the overview slide.
steps.instruction: Compare the hospital controller label on the diagram with the other listed components so the tote add/remove role is not confused with AGVs, tote tip stations, or OptiSweep Software.
steps.expected_result: The user distinguishes the hospital controller from the other architecture components shown on the slide.
steps.healthy_condition: The hospital controller is identified as a separate labeled component from AGVs, tote tip stations, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, Aveva HMI, and UPS Chat.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot distinguish the hospital controller from the other labeled components or assigns the tote add/remove role to another component without source support.

actions.label: Compare labeled components
actions.exact_action: Compare the hospital controller label on the diagram with the other listed components so the tote add/remove role is not confused with AGVs, tote tip stations, or OptiSweep Software.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Compare the hospital controller label against the other labeled core elements on the overview slide.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the role is being inferred for components not explicitly assigned that role in the source.
Escalate if the user needs authoritative functional boundaries for other components beyond this overview segment.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide shows UPS Chat, tote tipper or tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI.
This training slide presents the core elements of the OptiSweep environment.

steps.title: Record the identified component role
steps.purpose: Capture the source-supported conclusion for future reference.
steps.instruction: Record that the source assigns tote removal and tote addition activity to the hospital controller.
steps.expected_result: The user documents that the hospital controller is the source-identified component for tote removal and tote addition.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded note matches the source wording and does not add unsupported operating detail.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded note adds unsupported procedural detail or assigns the role to the wrong component.

actions.label: Document source-supported role
actions.exact_action: Record that the source assigns tote removal and tote addition activity to the hospital controller.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if documentation begins to include unsupported tote handling steps.
Escalate if a detailed operating or recovery procedure is requested.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We have a hospital controller for you to remove and add totes.
The hospital controller is described as the component used to remove and add totes.

success_criteria: The user identifies the hospital controller on the overview architecture slide.
The user verifies from the transcript that the hospital controller is the component used to remove and add totes.
The user does not confuse this role with AGVs, tote tip stations, or OptiSweep Software.
The recorded conclusion stays within the source-supported statement.
healthy_conditions: The overview slide is accessible and legible.
The transcript statement explicitly links the hospital controller to tote removal and tote addition.
The user treats this as a component-reference procedure rather than an operating procedure.
failure_conditions: The slide or transcript cannot be accessed or verified.
The hospital controller cannot be distinguished from other components on the overview slide.
The user attempts to derive unsupported tote handling steps from this source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate to SME review if a detailed tote add/remove operating procedure is needed.
Escalate if users need screen-level workflow, button presses, or controller interactions, because this source does not provide them.
Escalate if there is uncertainty about the responsibilities of other architecture components beyond the overview statement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overview slide showing the hospital controller as one of the core OptiSweep system elements.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: We have a hospital controller for you to remove and add totes.

missing_details: The source does not provide a detailed tote removal procedure.
The source does not provide a detailed tote addition procedure.
The source does not provide HMI screens, button presses, commands, or controller interactions for tote handling.
The source does not define escalation contacts or role boundaries beyond general operator use.
